Early Blight.meaning: Early blight is a fungal disease that affects tomato plants, causing dark brown lesions on the leaves and stems.
Early Blight.causes: Caused by the fungus Alternaria solani under warm, moist conditions.
Early Blight.symptoms: Dark, sunken spots on leaves and stems. Older leaves are affected first.
Early Blight.remedy: Can be minimized by maintaining optimum growing conditions, including proper fertilization, irrigation, and management of other pests.

Late Blight.meaning: Late blight is a severe fungal disease that rapidly destroys leaves, stems, and fruits, often appearing later in the growing season.
Late Blight.causes: Caused by the fungus Phytophthora infestans under cool, wet conditions.
Late Blight.symptoms: Water-soaked lesions on leaves and stems, dark brown lesions on fruits.
Late Blight.remedy: Can be treated with fungicides, proper harvesting and storage practices, and eliminating infected plants.

Leaf Mold.meaning: Leaf mold is a fungal disease that affects tomato plants, causing mold growth on the underside of leaves.
Leaf Mold.causes: Caused by the fungus Cladosporium fulvum, thriving in humid, moist environments.
Leaf Mold.symptoms: Yellowing of upper leaves, with grayish mold on the undersides, leading to leaf drop.
Leaf Mold.remedy: Can be controlled by improving air circulation, reducing humidity, and applying fungicides.

Healthy.meaning: Healthy tomato plant without any disease.
Healthy.symptoms: Normal plant appearance, with no unusual spots or wilting.
